key_findings: Three independent failure modes require three distinct detection mechanisms: schema failures (per-record streaming check), loss signatures (window-close count reconciliation), and continuity gaps (periodic heartbeat tick); unified approach misses window-level failures

Count reconciliation for loss detection (count_in - count_out - count_dlq - count_schema_quarantine) is schema-free and O(1) per window; avoids end-to-end trace which requires correlation ID injection at every record

Per-record schema check uses adaptive sampling: 100% for first 100 records per window, 10% thereafter; auto-escalates to 100% if quarantine rate exceeds 1% — caps CPU cost while catching schema bombs early
DLQ settle delay: read DLQ count at window_close + 5s to allow in-flight records to settle before loss_rate computation; prevents false loss detection during high-throughput bursts
Heartbeat-based continuity check (last_seen_ts comparison) requires zero schema changes; sequence numbering approach rejected for requiring monotonic ID injection at lane entry
Validator self-monitoring via self-heartbeat to ORS: VALIDATOR_DOWN alert if heartbeat absent for 2× interval — prevents silent validator failure
Fault injection suite: 7 fault types (missing field, type mismatch, silent loss, lane stall, DLQ flood, schema version change, window drift) — 100% detection required for CI gate
Hot-reloadable validator_config.json: all thresholds reload without restart; config_version stored with each alert for audit provenance

decisions.title: Streaming per-record schema check over batch validation
decisions.summary: Schema check on every record (with sampling policy) rather than at window close
decisions.rationale: Batch check at 60s window close allows 11,640 DNS records (194/sec × 60s) to be ingested with a schema regression before detection. Per-record streaming check detects on record 1. Sampling policy caps CPU to manageable levels while preserving fast detection.

decisions.title: Count reconciliation for loss detection over end-to-end tracing
decisions.summary: Loss computed as count_in - count_out - count_dlq - count_schema_quarantine at window close
decisions.rationale: End-to-end tracing requires correlation ID injection into every record schema — a breaking change. Count reconciliation is schema-free, O(1) per window, and naturally idempotent on replay.

decisions.title: Heartbeat-based continuity check over sequence numbering
decisions.summary: Continuity detected via last_seen_ts comparison against timeout threshold; no monotonic ID required
decisions.rationale: Sequence numbering requires stateful ID injection at lane entry — a coordination point and schema change. Heartbeat needs only a timestamp register, is resilient to record reordering, and requires zero schema changes.

decisions.title: Adaptive sampling policy (100% first 100, 10% thereafter, auto-escalate)
decisions.summary: First 100 records per window always validated; thereafter 10% sample; escalate to 100% on quarantine spike
decisions.rationale: 100% sampling at 700k/hr (194 records/sec) would require 194 schema checks/sec. 10% reduces to 19/sec — within latency budget. First-100 rule catches schema regressions at window open. Auto-escalation on quarantine spike catches mid-window failures.

decisions.title: Self-heartbeat emitted to ORS for validator liveness monitoring
decisions.summary: Validator emits VALIDATOR_HEARTBEAT every heartbeat_check_interval_sec; ORS alerts on absence
decisions.rationale: A validator that fails silently is worse than no validator (false sense of security). Self-heartbeat ensures the monitoring system itself is monitored.

check_modules.name: Loss Signature Check
check_modules.trigger: per-window, at window close + 5s settle delay

checks.name: pipeline_gap
checks.condition: HTTP window complete without DNS predecessor in lookback

ors_reflex_map.escalation: alert if drain fails within 2 windows

fault_injection_suite.method: dispatch record, discard before output, not DLQ
halt record production for 2x heartbeat timeout
write 60 records to DLQ in one window
inject v0-format record into v1-schema lane
slow window completion to 2x target duration

risk_model.title: DLQ read before settle produces inflated loss_rate
risk_model.mitigation: 5s settle delay after window close before DLQ count read

risk_model.title: Schema false alarms during tool version rollout
risk_model.mitigation: Schema version detection + grace period for first 100 records of v0→v1 transition

risk_model.title: High CPU from 100% schema check at 700k/hr
risk_model.mitigation: 10% sampling after first 100; escalate only on quarantine spike

risk_model.title: Heartbeat false alarm during scheduled maintenance
risk_model.mitigation: MAINTENANCE_WINDOW_START signal from ORS suppresses Module C alerts

risk_model.title: Validator self-failure undetected
risk_model.mitigation: Self-heartbeat to ORS; VALIDATOR_DOWN alert if heartbeat absent for 2x interval

risk_model.title: count_in/count_out drift under replay
risk_model.mitigation: Replay increments count_out only; count_in is immutable per original dispatch

risk_model.title: Config change produces alert storm
risk_model.mitigation: Config version stored with alert; ops correlates alert spike to config change timestamp

recommended_actions.action: Implement count_in counter in controller dispatch path — required for Module B loss reconciliation
Deploy Module A first (schema check) — immediate value; required fields + type validation on DNS records alone catches most regressions
Set DLQ settle delay to 5s in Module B — prevents false loss alarms on high-throughput bursts
Configure validator self-heartbeat to ORS from day 1 — ensures monitoring system is itself monitored
Run fault injection suite before production promotion — gate on >=95% recall across all 7 fault types
Add MAINTENANCE_WINDOW_START signal to ORS protocol — prevents heartbeat false alarms during planned downtime
Hot-reload validator_config.json on file change — enables threshold tuning without restart
Monitor validator execution latency as ORS signal — alert if schema_check_latency p95 > 50ms or window_close_check > 5s

assumptions: A1: Controller maintains count_in counter per dispatch — required for loss reconciliation
A2: DLQ is readable within 5s of record arrival (settle delay assumption)
A3: ORS signal bus available as alert emission target; accepts structured JSON
A4: Maintenance window signals sent by ORS before planned downtime
A5: Tool parser schema contracts (dnsx_v1, httpx_v1, nuclei_v1, katana_v1) are stable reference
A6: validator_config.json hot-reload via inotify or polling (<=5s reload latency)
